Text:
Built a YouTube UI Clone Using Only HTML & CSS.

Focused on replicating layout structure, sidebar navigation, video grid system, and responsive behavior using pure HTML and CSS. Strengthened my understanding of Flexbox, Grid, and clean UI structuring principles.

Successfully completed the Netflix Clone Bootcamp using HTML & CSS.

Gained hands-on experience building a real-world UI clone from scratch. Improved my understanding of responsive layouts, positioning, and structured styling.

Started my journey in full stack development.

Working with React, Python, Django, Node.js, Express, and MongoDB to understand how frontend and backend systems integrate in production-level applications.

Built a Tic-Tac-Toe game using JavaScript.

Implemented game logic, win detection, DOM manipulation, and reset functionality. This project strengthened my understanding of event handling and state management.

Recognized as a Galactic Problem Solver in the 2025 NASA Space Apps Challenge.

Collaborated on solving real-world challenges using innovation, teamwork, and technology-driven approaches.

Hackathon Project: AI-Powered DPR Quality & Risk Prediction.

Developed a system to analyze DPR documents and predict delays and cost overruns using AI/ML techniques.

Tech Stack: HTML, CSS, JavaScript, Python (Flask).

Attended NVIDIA RTX AI PC Day 2026.

Explored GPU acceleration, AI-powered workflows, and high-performance computing systems through hands-on demos and technical sessions.

Participated in a group discussion on Personal Psychology.

Discussed emotional intelligence, leadership psychology, self-awareness, and the importance of mental well-being in personal and professional growth.

Completed a data analysis mini project using Python.

Performed data cleaning, visualization, and exploratory data analysis to extract meaningful insights from raw datasets.

Learning advanced JavaScript concepts.

Currently exploring asynchronous programming, API integration, and real-world application logic to build scalable web applications.

Built a responsive portfolio website to showcase my projects.

Focused on clean UI, smooth navigation, and optimized layout for better user experience across devices.

Exploring Machine Learning fundamentals.

Studying supervised learning algorithms, model evaluation techniques, and practical implementation using Python libraries.

Contributed to an open-source project for the first time.

Learned about Git workflows, pull requests, issue tracking, and collaborative development practices.

Working on improving problem-solving skills through daily coding practice.

Solving data structures and algorithms problems consistently to strengthen logical thinking and prepare for technical interviews.
Looking for jobs on LinkedIn is like online dating: Full of promises, but in the end, you're just left ghosted.

LinkedIn scams be like: 'Congratulations, you've been selected for a role you didn’t even apply for!' 
 The catch? Pay Rs. 50,000 for the honor.

To everyone who's still looking for a job...

I see you. I feel you.

Every rejection email feels like a punch in the gut, and every 'We'll get back to you' sounds more like 'You'll never hear from us.'

But I want you to know, you're not alone in this.

According to a study, 80% of jobseekers struggle with anxiety and self-doubt during their search. It's normal to feel lost, but it's not the end.

Take breaks, breathe, and remember, this doesn't define you. Your worth is not tied to an offer letter.

Your mental health matters more than any job.

Sometimes, we forget that a company’s brand name doesn’t define someone’s talent. It’s easy to get caught up in the 'big company = big talent' mindset, but that's not always the case.

I’ve had the privilege of working with people from smaller companies (lesser known) who blow me away with their skills and dedication. They don’t need a fancy title or a famous brand behind them to prove their worth.

I've seen the other side too—people in top-tier companies feeling lost, overwhelmed, or stuck, even though the world sees them as 'successful'.

Let’s stop attaching someone’s value to the company they work for. Freshers especially need to hear this—skills are what matter, not the size of the company behind them.

At the end of the day, happiness and growth don’t come from a brand name, they come from doing what you love and constantly improving your craft.
